title: Samosa Crispy Potato Pastry

seo.title: Crispy Samosa Recipe: Authentic Indian Potato Pastry
seo.metaDescription: Master the art of making a crispy Samosa recipe at home! This authentic Indian potato pastry is perfect as an appetizer. Get step-by-step instructions for a delicious treat.
seo.h1: Authentic Crispy Samosa Recipe: Indian Potato Pastry
h2Headings: Pro Tips for Extra Crispy Samosas
seo.imageAlt: Plate of golden crispy samosas with potato filling, garnished with cilantro, served with chutney. Authentic Indian appetizer.
seo.imageCaption: These homemade crispy samosas are filled with a flavorful potato mixture, making them the ultimate Indian appetizer for any occasion!

description: These irresistible Samosas feature a perfectly crispy, golden-brown pastry shell encasing a warmly spiced potato and pea filling. A quintessential Indian appetizer, they offer a delightful crunch followed by a burst of aromatic flavors. Enjoy these savory parcels as a snack or starter, perfectly complemented by a tangy chutney.

instructions.title: Prepare the Dough
instructions.text: In a large bowl, combine all-purpose flour, ajwain, and 1/2 tsp salt. Add melted ghee or 1/4 cup oil and rub it into the flour until the mixture resembles breadcrumbs. Gradually add cold water, kneading to form a firm, smooth dough. Cover with a damp cloth and let it rest for 20 minutes.

instructions.title: Make the Filling Base
instructions.text: Heat 2 tablespoons of oil in a pan over medium heat. Add cumin seeds and let them splutter. Add grated ginger and finely chopped green chili, sauté for 30 seconds until fragrant. Stir in coriander powder, turmeric powder, and red chili powder, cooking for another 30 seconds.

instructions.title: Combine Filling Ingredients
instructions.text: Add the boiled and coarsely mashed potatoes and green peas to the pan. Mix well, ensuring the spices coat the potatoes evenly. Season with salt to taste, garam masala, and amchur. Cook for 5-7 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the flavors meld. Remove from heat and stir in fresh cilantro. Let the filling cool completely.

instructions.title: Roll and Shape the Dough
instructions.text: Divide the rested dough into 8-10 equal portions. Roll each portion into an oval shape, about 6-7 inches long and 3-4 inches wide. Cut each oval in half lengthwise, creating two semi-circles. This will form the base for your samosa cones.

instructions.title: Form the Samosas
instructions.text: Take one semi-circle, moisten the straight edge lightly with water. Bring the two ends of the straight edge together to form a cone. Press the seam firmly to seal. Fill the cone with 1-2 tablespoons of the cooled potato mixture. Moisten the top edges of the cone with water, then bring them together and press to seal, creating a triangle shape. Ensure all seams are tightly sealed.

instructions.title: Deep Fry the Samosas
instructions.text: Heat 3-4 cups of vegetable oil in a deep pan or kadai over medium-low heat. The oil should be moderately hot (around 300-325°F or 150-160°C), not smoking. Carefully slide a few samosas into the hot oil, ensuring not to overcrowd the pan. Frying in batches will help maintain oil temperature.

instructions.title: Achieve Golden Perfection
instructions.text: Fry the samosas on medium-low heat, turning occasionally, for about 12-15 minutes or until they are golden brown and crispy on all sides. The low heat is crucial for achieving a perfectly cooked interior and a flaky, crispy crust without burning the exterior.

instructions.title: Drain and Serve
instructions.text: Once fried to perfection, remove the samosas with a slotted spoon and place them on a plate lined with paper towels to drain excess oil. Serve hot with your favorite Indian chutneys like tangy tamarind chutney or refreshing mint chutney for an authentic experience.

tips: For the crispiest Samosas, knead a firm dough and ensure the oil is not too hot when frying. Frying on medium-low heat allows the pastry to cook through and become flaky without burning.
Do not over-mash the potatoes for the filling; leave some small chunks for texture. Adding a pinch of roasted crushed fennel seeds to the filling can enhance the aroma and authenticity.

Samosas can be prepared ahead of time and frozen before frying. Arrange them on a baking sheet to freeze individually, then transfer to a freezer-safe bag. Fry from frozen, adding a few extra minutes to the cooking time.
article: Samosa Crispy Potato Pastry is a beloved Indian Appetizers dish enjoyed across Indian. This authentic recipe brings traditional flavors to your kitchen.
